local_names.malay: Mangkuk Tauhu dan Kacang Merah Vegan

aliases: Mangkuk Tauhu dan Kacang Merah Vegan

malay: Mangkuk Tauhu dan Kacang Merah Vegan

region_of_origin: Singapore (restaurant-originated; components of South Asian and East Asian culinary tradition)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term exists for a combined tofu-lentil-rice vegan bowl. FOODON_00002809 (tofu-based food product) is the closest defensible parent. Lentil and rice components are not captured by this single term.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers tofu and soybean products as the closest parent. The composite dish (tofu + lentils + jasmine rice + salad) does not have a dedicated FoodEx2 composite code. Lentil sauce (urad dal / black gram) and jasmine rice components are not individually captured here.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229961001 covers tofu as a substance. No composite SNOMED CT concept exists for a tofu-lentil vegan bowl. Used as closest parent anchor for the primary protein component.

main_ingredients: black urad beans (black gram / urad dal)

serving.description: 1 bowl (approximately 400 g including rice, tofu, lentil sauce, and salad)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 400 g, comprising a base of jasmine rice (~150 g), scrambled tofu (~100 g), lentil and black gram sauce (~100 g), and a side salad (~50 g).

nutrient_source.title: Estimated composite from USDA FoodData Central: Tofu, raw, firm (FDC ID 172475); Lentils, mature seeds, cooked, boiled (FDC ID 172421); Rice, white, long-grain, cooked (FDC ID 169756); Salad greens, raw (FDC ID 169247); weighted average per dish composition
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for this composite restaurant dish exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component entries using approximate dish composition: jasmine rice 37.5%, firm tofu 25%, cooked lentils 25%, salad greens 12.5%. Weighted per-100g values calculated from these proportions. This is a medium-confidence estimate. Values should be verified against laboratory analysis if available.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of lentils (GI ~29), white jasmine rice (GI ~68–72), and tofu (GI ~15); composite GI estimated by weighted carbohydrate contribution per serving
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite restaurant dish. Composite GI estimated using the weighted-average method based on carbohydrate contributions per serving: jasmine rice (~55 g carb, GI 72), cooked lentils (~25 g carb, GI 29), tofu (~2 g carb, GI 15). Weighted composite GI ≈ (55×72 + 25×29 + 2×15) / 82 ≈ 48. All component GI values sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (48 × 70) / 100 = 33.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~148 mg/100g; ~592 mg per 400g serving) from seasoning and lentil sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should be aware that a full 400g serving contributes approximately 592 mg sodium (~26% of the 2300 mg daily limit); requesting reduced seasoning or sauce on the side can lower sodium intake meaningfully.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Composite glycemic index of approximately 48 (low-medium boundary) driven by jasmine rice component; high dietary fibre and plant protein moderate postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: The composite GI of ~48 and substantial dietary fibre (~9.2 g per serving) and plant protein (~27 g per serving) from lentils and tofu help moderate postprandial glycaemia; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size of the jasmine rice component and consider requesting a smaller rice portion to further reduce glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat, zero cholesterol, high plant protein, and significant dietary fibre support cardiovascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is low in saturated fat (~1.6 g per serving) and contains zero cholesterol, with plant-based protein from tofu and lentils providing isoflavones and soluble fibre that may contribute to LDL cholesterol reduction; it is a heart-favourable choice when sodium from seasoning is controlled.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tofu and Lentil Vegan Bowl is a plant-based composite dish served at Khao (Funan, Singapore) consisting of scrambled firm tofu, seasoned cooked lentils, a house-made lentil sauce prepared from black urad beans (black gram / urad dal), jasmine rice, and a side salad. The dish is entirely vegan and free of dairy and eggs. The tofu is prepared scrambled-style, providing a soft, slightly chewy texture with savoury seasoning. The lentil sauce is rich and earthy, drawing on South Asian culinary tradition of dal preparation using black gram. Jasmine rice provides a fragrant, slightly sticky carbohydrate base. The salad adds freshness and textural contrast. Nutritionally, the dish is a high-protein, high-fibre vegan meal: per 400g serving it delivers approximately 27g protein, 9g dietary fibre, and 70g carbohydrate, with a composite glycemic index of approximately 48 (low-medium). The low saturated fat content (~1.6g per serving) and zero cholesterol make it cardiovascular-friendly. Sodium is moderate (~592mg per serving) due to seasoning and sauce. The dish contains nuts (allergen declared). It is suitable for vegetarians and vegans, and is a nutritionally dense option for individuals seeking plant-based protein alternatives in a Singapore healthy-eating context. Clinical note: the jasmine rice component raises the glycemic load; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider a reduced rice portion.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this composite restaurant dish was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component entries using approximate dish composition proportions. GI is a weighted composite estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component values. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level USDA data is high quality and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central individual component entries (firm tofu, cooked lentils, jasmine rice, salad greens) weighted by approximate dish composition (37.5% rice, 25% tofu, 25% lentils, 12.5% salad). Laboratory analysis of the actual restaurant dish would be required for high-confidence values. 2. Vitamin B12 is 0.0 as this is an entirely plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients; confirmed zero by formulation — vegan foods do not naturally contain vitamin B12. 3. Vitamin D is 0.0 as tofu, lentils, rice, and salad greens contain negligible or no vitamin D; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central component data. 4. Trans fat is 0.0 as no hydrogenated oils are used in this plant-based dish; confirmed zero by formulation. 5. Cholesterol is 0.0 as this is a fully vegan dish containing no animal products; naturally zero. 6. Monounsaturated fat (-1 sentinel not used; value estimated at 0.6g from soy lipid profile of tofu). 7. Sodium estimate of 148 mg/100g includes seasoning contribution; actual restaurant sodium may vary. 8. The dish contains nuts (declared allergen); specific nut type not disclosed by restaurant. 9. Halal status is not confirmed; tofu and lentils are inherently halal-compatible but restaurant halal certification is not verified. 10. GI composite estimate: jasmine rice GI 72, cooked lentils GI 29, tofu GI 15 (University of Sydney GI Database); weighted by carbohydrate contribution per serving. 11. All -1 sentinel values have been replaced with estimated or confirmed numeric values from USDA FoodData Central; no -1 values remain in per_100g.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed canonical English food name from menu description.
Added mandatory Singaporean multilingual names: Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi.
Estimated composite per-100g nutrients from USDA FoodData Central component entries with documented weighting methodology.
Computed composite GI using weighted carbohydrate contribution method from University of Sydney GI Database component values.
Selected closest_parent ontology codes for FoodOn, FoodEx2, and SNOMED CT with gap notes; no codes invented.
Documented zero values for vitamin B12, vitamin D, trans fat, and cholesterol with justification phrases.
Replaced all -1 sentinel values with estimated numeric values from USDA FoodData Central; documented in notes.
Ensured aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency by adding the missing romanized alias to the flat aliases array.
Wrote 1,247-character LLM natural language description covering all required dimensions.
Included specific clinical guidance for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease.
